function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i < 256' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i < 256' always executed?
Can the loop with condition 'j < 255' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'j < 255' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 2:  <statement> int main (void) </statement> is reached, the statement line 7:  <statement> printf ("extern int "); </statement> has already been reached?

Is it guaranteed that whenever line 11:  <statement> for (j = 0; j < 256; ++j) </statement> is reached, the statement line 10:  <statement> printf ("foo%02xff (void);\nint bar%02x (int x) { return x", i, i); </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable i at line 5: <statement>   for (i = 0; i < 256; ++i) </statement> depend on the value of variable j at line 4: <statement>   int i, j; </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'i' live at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'j' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'printf ("foo%02xff (void);\nint bar%02x (int x) { return x", i, i);' be considered dead code?
Is this statement not dead: “for (i = 0; i < 256; ++i)”?
